hero_subtitle: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media screening results from multiple providers into structured workflows that enable efficient review, escalation, and audit-ready compliance evidence.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Sanctions alerts create manual review bottlenecks
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to route and escalate sanctions and adverse media screening results that come from multiple disconnected providers. This leads to duplicated efforts, slow case handling, and a weak audit trail that complicates regulatory review and response.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects multiple sanctions and adverse media provider results into structured review workflows that automate alert triage, case creation, escalation tracking and audit-ready evidence for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From sanctions alerts to case resolution
workflow_description: SFTY routes sanctions and adverse media screening results through risk rules, review workflows, case creation, decision logging and audit-ready evidence preservation.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond sanctions alerts. One escalation layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media outputs into structured workflows, case evidence and audit-ready decisions without disrupting existing screening providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing sanctions screening by preserving evidentiary records and controlling provider outputs within scalable, enterprise-grade compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions screening?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures sanctions and adverse media screening results into one review, case management and escalation workflow for VASP compliance teams.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY route sanctions alerts?
faq_2_answer: SFTY normalizes multiple sanctions outputs, applies rules, routes alerts for manual review or escalation and documents every step in the workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve screening evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready logs of screening results, reviewer decisions, evidence packages and case history for ongoing compliance evidence and supervisory review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing sanctions providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects with multiple sanctions, PEP and adverse media providers without requiring replacement, connecting their outputs into one operational layer.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions screening tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace sanctions tools but integrates their outputs into structured workflows and supports compliance decision documentation.
final_title: Connect sanctions alerts into structured case workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY routes sanctions and adverse media screening results into controlled reviews, escalations and audit-ready evidence packages.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
